entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species used in the study to examine pre-harvest physiology and germinability

entities.name: GERMINABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of sorghum grains to germinate, measured as percentage germination

entities.name: WATER ABSORPTION
entities.description: The process of water uptake by sorghum grains during their development

entities.name: ELECTRICAL CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of ion leakage from grain leachates, used to assess physiological state

entities.name: GRAIN DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The physiological process of grain growth and maturation in sorghum

entities.name: GRAIN FRESH WEIGHT
entities.description: The total weight of the grain including its water content

entities.name: GRAIN DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the grain after the removal of water

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The percentage of water present in the sorghum grain

entities.name: ANTHESIS
entities.description: The period during which a flower is fully open and functional, used as a temporal marker for grain development

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: The diverse set of 47 germplasm lines and 3 standard cultivars of sorghum evaluated

relationships.description: Sorghum is the crop studied for germinability
Sorghum is the crop studied for water absorption
Sorghum is the crop studied for electrical conductivity
Variation in germinability was observed among genotypes
Variation in water absorption was observed among genotypes
Variation in electrical conductivity was observed among genotypes
Variation in fresh weight was observed among genotypes
Variation in dry weight was observed among genotypes
Variation in moisture content was observed among genotypes
Grain development is measured in days after anthesis
Germination timing is measured relative to anthesis
Water uptake is measured at 40 days after anthesis
